title: skills.yaml
description: Skill manifest. Each entry declares a canonical skill ID, source path, supported products, required nils-cli binaries with semver floors, optional aliases, and per-product path overrides. Plan 03 (`03-domain-migration-reporting-pilot`) populates the `skills:` list — Plan 01 leaves it empty.

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. Drift audit uses this to pick the validator.

skills.description: List of skill entries. Empty in Plan 01.

id.description: Canonical `<domain>.<skill>` identifier (lowercase, hyphenated within each segment, dot-separated).

domain.description: Owning domain (matches the first segment of `id`).

products.description: Per-product render configuration. Each entry maps a supported product (`codex` / `claude`) to its product-native name and rendered output path.

required_clis.description: Map of nils-cli binary name to a semver range (e.g. `">=0.5.0"`). The literal placeholder `<TBD: pin during Phase 1>` is allowed only during Phase 1; Plan 02 onwards treats any surviving `<TBD>` as a gate failure.

state_out_mode.description: Default `runtime` (agent-out resolves the path at execution); `literal` only when the consuming context cannot exec.

aliases.description: Optional aliases keyed by product, used when a skill has different invocation names per product but the same behaviour.

divergent.description: Set to `true` when a skill exists in both products but with divergent behaviour. Drift audit warns on every divergent entry.

portability_notes.description: Free-text notes about portability constraints, host requirements, or migration provenance.

name.description: Product-native invocation name (e.g. Claude's `<plugin>:<skill>`, Codex's slash variant).

path_override.description: Optional override for the product-native source path (e.g. legacy `tools/market-research/<x>` placement).
